findings.title: Involuntary session-expiry (401) path leaves the previous user's trips/expenses/days/budgets in STATE and re-persists them to localStorage — shared-device privacy leak
findings.rootCause: The deliberate logout() in pages/profile.ts:70-101 was carefully hardened (R2 / SY7 / SY9 / R8-B3) to null out user, activeTripId, trips, archivedTrips, expenses, tripDays, budgets, activities, photos, notifications, savedFormats, categories AND clearOutbox(). But the INVOLUNTARY teardown path — apiFetch()'s 401 branch (core.ts:269-284) — only calls clearAuthToken() + sets STATE.user=null + emit. clearAuthToken() (core.ts:82-157) removes the localStorage blob and resets notifications/draftExpense/geminiApiKey/settlements/achievements/lastImportBatch/preferences/rateMode/insightCurrency, but it does NOT clear trips/archivedTrips/expenses/tripDays/budgets/activities/photos/savedFormats/categories or the outbox. Because saveState (subscribed to state:changed, state.ts:438) serializes the WHOLE STATE object 250 ms after the emit, the just-removed blob is immediately repopulated with the previous user's data. The SW cache IS cleared (CLEAR_API_CACHE / CLEAR_USER are posted from clearAuthToken), which is why this gap was missed — only the in-memory + localStorage STATE leak remains.

findings.title: isCreator omitted from /api/auth/google login response → Creator tab/card invisible to non-admin Creators until a full page reload
findings.rootCause: The login response in /api/auth/google (auth.py:259-278) builds the `user` object WITHOUT an `isCreator` field, whereas /api/user-status (the boot/reload probe, auth.py:64-84) computes and returns `isCreator`. handleGoogleLogin sets `STATE.user = data.user` (auth.ts:58), so after a fresh login STATE.user.isCreator is undefined. isCreatorUser() = `!!STATE.user?.isCreator || isAdminUser()` (Settings.tsx:78-80) is therefore false for a non-admin creator. Neither pullFromServer nor /api/data re-hydrate STATE.user or carry is_creator, so the value stays undefined until the next boot calls restoreSession()→/api/user-status, which is the only place isCreator is populated.

findings.title: auth_sessions rows are never reaped once their JWT expires (only revoked rows are swept) → Sessions UI lists phantom dead devices and the table grows unbounded
findings.rootCause: issue_token()→_create_session() inserts a NEW auth_sessions row on every single login (auth.py:176-216) and nothing ever deletes a session purely because its JWT expired. list_sessions (auth.py:357-363) selects `WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL` with NO created_at/expiry filter, so every never-revoked row is returned regardless of whether its token is still valid. The background sweep in _cleanup_feed_orphans (main.py:795-798) only deletes rows where `revoked_at IS NOT NULL AND revoked_at < now-30d` — it never touches expired-but-unrevoked rows. (Most logouts revoke just the current session and most expirations revoke nothing, so the un-revoked population dominates.)

findings.title: Settings → Sessions panel is entirely hardcoded English (no t() calls) — es/fr/pt users see an untranslated device-management screen
findings.rootCause: Sessions.tsx self-documents the gap at lines 118-120 ('keys for these strings can be added to the locale tables once the SessionsView ships — for now we hard-code English'). All visible strings are string literals rather than t(...) lookups, and no corresponding keys exist in locales/{en,es,fr,pt}.ts. The relative-time helper _formatRelativeTime DOES use the i18n table (feed.relTime*), which makes the surrounding hardcoded strings inconsistent.

findings.title: Sessions UI device-label re-parser (and its docstring) assume a raw User-Agent, but the server now stores a pre-summarized label — the regex parse is dead code that survives only by accidental fallback
findings.rootCause: The server changed (R2 audit, auth.py:135-173) to store a summarized 'Browser on OS' string via _summarize_ua() in _create_session (auth.py:192), specifically to avoid leaking the full UA fingerprint. The client (_deviceLabel) and the misc.ts doc comment were never updated to match — they still treat deviceLabel as a verbatim UA. Because the summary contains no 'Browser/version' token, the parse produces browser='' and os='Unknown' and returns the raw summary, so it only works by coincidence; any future change to the summary format (or a label like 'Opera on Linux' that contains an OS keyword) would render a wrong/garbled label.

findings.title: initGoogleLogin() renders the GIS button into a #googleBtnContainer element that does not exist anywhere — dead/misleading boot code
findings.rootCause: initGoogleLogin (auth.ts:160-171) polls for the GIS SDK and then does `const container = document.getElementById('googleBtnContainer'); if (container) renderButton(...)`. The only login button container that exists is #loginWallBtnContainer, created by LoginWall.tsx:141. So the container lookup always returns null and the renderButton never runs from this path; initGoogleLogin's only live effect is the early ensureGsiInitialized() callback wiring. The dead branch reads as if a second button mount point exists.
